stem: In a right-angled triangle, the hypotenuse is $10$ cm and one angle is $30°$. Find the length of the side opposite the $30°$ angle. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
In a right-angled triangle, the hypotenuse is $15$ cm and one angle is $40°$. Find the length of the side adjacent to the $40°$ angle. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
In a right-angled triangle, the hypotenuse is $8$ m and one angle is $55°$. Find the length of the side opposite the $55°$ angle. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
In a right-angled triangle, the hypotenuse is $20$ m and one angle is $25°$. Find the length of the side adjacent to the $25°$ angle. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite a $30°$ angle is $6$ cm. Find the length of the hypotenuse. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
In a right-angled triangle, the side adjacent to a $35°$ angle is $10$ m. Find the length of the side opposite the $35°$ angle. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
In a right-angled triangle, the side adjacent to a $42°$ angle is $9$ cm. Find the length of the hypotenuse. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite a $50°$ angle is $7$ m. Find the length of the adjacent side. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.

A flagpole is $8$ m tall. A support wire runs from the top of the flagpole to a point on the ground, making an angle of $35°$ with the horizontal ground. Find the length of the support wire. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
hint: The flagpole is the opposite side and the wire is the hypotenuse. Use SOH.
explanation: The flagpole (opposite) is $8$ m and the wire (hypotenuse) makes a $35°$ angle with the ground. Using SOH: $\sin 35° = \dfrac{8}{w}$, so $w = \dfrac{8}{\sin 35°} = \dfrac{8}{0.5736\ldots} = 13.95$ m.

stem: A ramp is $15$ m long and makes an angle of $20°$ with the horizontal ground. Find the vertical height gained by walking up the full length of the ramp. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
hint: The ramp is the hypotenuse and the vertical height is the opposite side. Use SOH.
explanation: The ramp (hypotenuse) is $15$ m and the angle with the ground is $20°$. The vertical height is the opposite side. Using SOH: $\sin 20° = \dfrac{h}{15}$, so $h = 15 \times \sin 20° = 15 \times 0.3420\ldots = 5.13$ m.

stem: A right-angled triangle has an angle of $48°$. The side adjacent to this angle is $11$ cm. Find the length of the side opposite the $48°$ angle. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
hint: You know the adjacent side and need the opposite side. Use TOA.

stem: A boat is $200$ m from the base of a cliff. The angle from the boat up to the top of the cliff is $28°$. Find the height of the cliff. Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
hint: The horizontal distance is the adjacent side and the cliff height is the opposite side. Use TOA.
explanation: The horizontal distance (adjacent) is $200$ m and the angle is $28°$. The cliff height is the opposite side. Using TOA: $\tan 28° = \dfrac{h}{200}$, so $h = 200 \times \tan 28° = 200 \times 0.5317\ldots = 106.34$ m.
